idea: Majority-class baseline (predict most frequent Pitch_ID)
notes: establish score floor

idea: MFCC+spectral features -> LightGBM multiclass, StratifiedKFold(3)
notes: primary baseline model | probe on 300-train/100-test subsample (65/82 classes stratifiable, n_jobs=1, 40 estimators): OOF acc=0.117 vs majority baseline 0.024 -> strong positive signal, promote to full CV | Full 82-class 3-fold CV, all 2330 train files, Kaggle kernel (4 vCPU, ~24min feature extraction). OOF acc=0.5682, but fold accs [0.716, 0.713, 0.276] -- fold 2 collapses, std=0.207 >> 0.02 CV variance threshold. High variance likely from small-class stratification (min 3/class) interacting with LightGBM multiclass on 82 classes.

idea: Adversarial validation train-vs-test on audio features
notes: check CV trustworthiness per prediction | probe (300 train/100 test subsample) adversarial train-vs-test AUC=0.973 (threshold 0.6); delta recorded as AUC-0.6 to signal strong distribution shift -> promote to full-scale adversarial check before trusting any plain StratifiedKFold CV number. Top separators: flatness_std, contrast_1/2/3_mean, mfcc_3_mean, zcr_mean/std, chroma_cqt features -- looks spectral/timbral, not just class-imbalance artifact. | Full-scale adversarial validation (train n=2330 vs test n=583, all real features): AUC=0.9984 (vs probe 0.973) -- confirms severe, non-noise train/test distribution shift. Top separators: flatness_std, zcr_std, mfcc_3_mean, contrast_1_mean/contrast_0_std/contrast_2_mean -- spectral/timbral loudness-like shift, consistent with probe finding. Plain StratifiedKFold CV on train is NOT trustworthy as an LB proxy; exp_004 (per-clip normalization) is the direct mitigation, and any submission decision must weight this risk (C13: probe before commit).

idea: Per-clip loudness/spectral normalization to close train/test gap (test has ~1.5x higher spectral contrast, ~0.5x lower ZCR and chroma_cqt means than train per probe adversarial-validation breakdown) -- add per-file z-scoring of amplitude before feature extraction, or normalize contrast/zcr features by per-clip RMS/percentile stats
notes: follow-up to exp_003 probe finding (AUC 0.973); train/test differ systematically on contrast_1-3_mean, zcr_mean/std, chroma_cqt_8/11_mean -- looks like recording-level loudness/noise-floor difference, not just class distribution | probe (300/100 subsample, N_SPLITS=2, 65 stratifiable classes): plain OOF=0.1168 vs RMS-normalized (target RMS 0.1) OOF=0.1512, delta +0.0344 -- matches predicted_delta 0.03, promote to full | exp_004 full CV OOF 0.70 vs exp_002 unnorm 0.5682 (delta +0.1318), matches probe direction. IMPORTANT: adversarial train/test AUC barely moved (0.9984 unnorm -> 0.9983 norm) -- normalization improved accuracy but did NOT close the distribution shift gap. Hypothesis that RMS-normalization fixes the shift is falsified; treat as an accuracy-improving feature transform independent of the shift problem.

idea: Pitch-candidate harmonic-evidence grid features (own implementation): for a grid of candidate f0s, score harmonic energy presence (STFT log-magnitude at each candidate's harmonics) + autocorrelation/cepstrum lag agreement, on RMS-normalized audio -- targets the missing-fundamental cue directly instead of generic spectral/chroma stats. Motivated by gemini's cross-reviewed sub_018 (CV 0.968, own review in .ai/reviews/claude-reviews-gemini-sub_018.md) converging on the same normalization + a similar grid-harmonic feature family independently reaching much higher CV than either agent's generic-feature LGBM baseline.
notes: queued after cross-review of gemini sub_018; probe on same 300/100 subsample before committing full extraction | probe (same 300/100 subsample as exp_002/exp_004, N_SPLITS=2, 65 stratifiable classes, own-implementation MIDI-grid harmonic/ACF/cepstrum features, 405 feats, RMS-normalized): OOF=0.5876 vs exp_002 plain baseline 0.1168 (delta +0.4708) and vs exp_004 normalized-librosa 0.1512 (delta +0.4364). Massive signal, far exceeds predicted_delta 0.15 -- promote to full immediately, this dwarfs the normalization-only gain. | exp_005 full CV OOF 0.9086 vs exp_002 unnorm 0.5682, fold std 0.0147 (stable, vs exp_002's 0.207). MIDI-grid harmonic/ACF/cepstrum pitch features, RMS-normalized. Own implementation (not copied from gemini).

idea: Shift-aware validation: reweight/holdout CV using exp_003's adversarial classifier probabilities (train-vs-test AUC 0.998) so validation folds are scored on the most test-like train samples, not a uniform random split -- directly addresses coordinator PLAN.md priority 1 (CV-LB sign agreement only 54% on gemini's 14 submissions).
notes: methodology fix, not an accuracy experiment -- success is measured by whether it produces a CV number that tracks LB better on the next few submissions, not by an OOF delta | Full shift-aware CV on exp_005 grid features: adversarial train-vs-test AUC 0.9724 (grid features), plain OOF 0.9064, most-test-like-quartile OOF 0.9160, least-test-like-quartile OOF 0.8834, gap(plain - most-test-like)=-0.0095. Unlike gemini's generic-feature shift (AUC~0.998, large CV-LB gaps per PLAN.md priority 1), the grid-harmonic features show almost no accuracy gap between test-like and non-test-like train rows -- reassuring, not a negative finding. Delta recorded as 0.0 since this is a validation-methodology check, not an accuracy change. Actionable: for grid-only models the plain CV number already looks fairly trustworthy; the shift-aware reweighting matters most for blends that still lean on shift-prone generic-feature models (see PLAN_DRAFT.md blend-weight idea).

idea: Combine MIDI-grid harmonic/ACF/cepstrum features (exp_005) with RMS-normalized generic librosa spectral/chroma features (exp_004) in one LGBM model -- test whether the generic features add complementary signal on top of the grid features or are now redundant.
notes: follow-up per STRATEGY.md Next Experiments; probe on 300/100 subsample first (C4/C13) | probe (300/100 subsample, N_SPLITS=2, 65 classes): grid-only OOF=0.5876, combined (grid+norm-librosa, 535 feats) OOF=0.5258, delta -0.0619 vs grid-only. Adding generic spectral/chroma features on top of the grid-harmonic features HURTS at this scale -- likely dilutes LGBM split budget with less-informative features / small-sample overfit (291 rows, 65 classes). Not promoting to full; grid-only features remain the better set.

idea: Ensemble grid-harmonic features (exp_005) across model families: LGBM + MLP + linear SVM (soft-vote), addressing C7 no-monoculture -- we've only tried LGBM on the grid features so far, and gemini's MLP/SVM blends found complementary signal on a weaker (generic-spectral) feature set.
notes: Probe (300/100 subsample, N_SPLITS=2, 65 classes): LGBM-only OOF=0.5876 (exp_005 ref) vs LGBM+MLP+SVM soft-vote OOF=0.5911 (delta +0.0034). MLP alone is far worse than LGBM at this tiny scale (probe_mlp_grid.py: MLP OOF=0.2096, delta -0.378 vs LGBM) -- 291 rows/65 classes is too little for a 128x64 MLP. Ensemble still edges out LGBM-only slightly (soft-vote lets LGBM dominate when MLP/SVM are weak), but the gain is small and within probe noise given only ~4-5 samples/class in the probe fold. Below predicted_delta 0.02 but positive -- promoting to full per C13 threshold (delta>0), though expect the gain to be marginal at full scale too since exp_005's full LGBM-only OOF is already 0.9086 (much less headroom for ensembling to help than at probe scale). | KILLED at ~15min wall clock, no fold completed. SVC(kernel='linear', probability=True) one-vs-one strategy for 82 classes creates ~3321 pairwise binary classifiers per fold, each running internal Platt-scaling CV (probability=True) -- badly underestimated cost for a probe delta that was only +0.0034 on a tiny 291-row/65-class probe sample (well within noise). Also starving CPU from exp_010 (2-CPU box, 3 heavy processes incl. gemini's job). Not worth a rewrite (e.g. drop probability=True, use decision_function calibration only, or cap SVM to fewer classes) given the marginal signal -- pivoting to exp_011 (shift-aware blend weighting) instead, which reuses exp_005/exp_006 outputs without new heavy model fits.

idea: Class-balanced LGBM on grid features: class_weight/sample_weight inversely proportional to class frequency (classes range 3-56 samples), test whether it helps minority-class recall without hurting overall OOF accuracy.
notes: Probe (300/100 subsample, N_SPLITS=2, 65 classes, same setup as exp_005/exp_007): class_weight='balanced' OOF acc=0.5567 vs plain 0.5876 (delta -0.0309). balanced_accuracy also dropped (0.5252 vs 0.5370, delta -0.0118) -- balancing hurts BOTH metrics at probe scale, not just overall accuracy. Falsifies predicted_delta +0.015. Not promoting to full; LGBM native handling of the 3-56/class imbalance via num_leaves/min_child_samples appears adequate; reweighting seems to overcorrect on 3-sample classes.

idea: LGBM hyperparameter probe on grid features: more estimators (200->500) + shallower num_leaves + higher min_child_samples to reduce overfit risk on rare classes; simple tuning pass on exp_005's feature set.
notes: Probe (300/100 subsample, N_SPLITS=2, 65 classes): baseline (n_est=40,leaves=15,min_child=2) OOF=0.5876 vs tuned (n_est=200,leaves=7,min_child=5) OOF=0.6667, delta +0.0790 -- exceeds predicted_delta 0.01 by far. Shallower trees + more estimators + higher min_child_samples helps a lot at probe scale (likely reduces overfit on 4-5 samples/class). Promote to full CV on exp_005 full features. | Full CV (StratifiedKFold3, n=2330, exp_005 grid feats): baseline(n_est=300,leaves=31,min_child=2)=0.9090 vs tuned(n_est=500,leaves=7,min_child=5)=0.9047. Probe predicted +0.0790, full measured -0.0043 -- full-scale reversal, shallower trees (leaves=7) underfit once real data volume is available; probe's 300-row subsample favored regularization that full 2330-row data doesn't need. Not promoting; keep exp_005 baseline config (submitted as sub_020).

idea: Shift-aware blend-weight search: instead of optimizing ensemble weights (LGBM+MLP+SVM from exp_008, or grid-vs-generic feature families) via COBYLA on plain OOF accuracy, score candidate weight vectors on exp_006's adversarially-weighted/most-test-like-quartile accuracy instead -- directly tests the PLAN_DRAFT.md hypothesis that gemini's sub_021 blend over-weights shift-prone generic-feature models because its optimizer is blind to shift risk.
notes: Reuses exp_006's adversarial P(is_test) classifier and exp_008's OOF probability arrays once it finishes; queued behind exp_008/exp_010 full runs. | Blend-weight grid search (w*grid_proba + (1-w)*generic_proba), full data, 3-fold: BOTH plain OOF accuracy and shift-aware (adversarial-p-test-weighted) accuracy are monotonically increasing in w, maximized at w=1.0 (pure grid, no generic blend at all). Falsifies the specific PLAN_DRAFT hypothesis that plain-OOF optimization would pick a worse (lower) w than shift-aware scoring -- here they agree exactly. But it strongly confirms the broader lesson from cross-reviewing gemini's sub_021: blending a shift-prone generic-feature model into a shift-robust grid model has NO rescuing value at any weight, under any scoring method measured. Delta vs current best (exp_005 grid-only, already w=1.0-equivalent) is 0 -- this is a methodology/confirmation result, not a new accuracy gain. Actionable: do not blend grid with normalized-generic features in any future ensemble; if ensembling, ensemble within the grid/shift-robust family only (e.g. exp_008's LGBM+MLP direction, minus the SVM cost blowup).

idea: exp_012: higher-fidelity LGBM num_leaves probe on grid features (full 2330-row/82-class data, single stratified 80/20 holdout instead of 3-fold, to fix exp_010's small-subsample probe/full mismatch) -- test num_leaves in {31 baseline, 63, 127} at fixed n_estimators=300
notes: exp_010 full-CV falsified the tiny-subsample probe direction (shallower leaves hurt at scale, not helped); this probe raises fidelity by keeping full row/class coverage and cutting fold count instead of row count, per scheduler's own note after exp_010. | Higher-fidelity probe (full 2330-row/82-class data, single stratified 80/20 holdout): baseline leaves=31 acc=0.9013, leaves=63 acc=0.8948 (delta -0.0064), leaves=127 acc=0.8970 (delta -0.0043). Both MORE capacity directions also hurt, same direction as exp_010's full-CV finding that LESS capacity (leaves=7) hurt. Baseline num_leaves=31 is a local optimum in both directions on grid features -- close off num_leaves as a tuning lever for this feature set; do not re-probe this axis (C9). Also a process lesson: cost badly underestimated (predicted 0.15hr/9min, actual 2569s=43min for 3 single-holdout configs) -- num_leaves cost scales worse than linear for 82-class multiclass LGBM (more leaves = more per-class split-finding work x82 classes); size cost estimates for multiclass LGBM configs accordingly next time.

idea: LGBM+MLP-only soft-vote on grid features (SVM dropped, per exp_008's cost blowup at full scale)
notes: isolates whether exp_008's tiny +0.0034 ensemble gain survives without SVM | Probe (300/100 subsample, N_SPLITS=2, 65 classes): LGBM-only OOF=0.5876, LGBM+MLP soft-vote (SVM dropped) OOF=0.5842, delta -0.0034. Negative -- exp_008's tiny +0.0034 gain was entirely due to SVM's contribution, not MLP. MLP alone drags the ensemble down slightly even in soft-vote. Killing this direction: within-family ensembling (LGBM+MLP, no SVM) does not help on grid features. SVM is too costly to run at full scale (82-class OvO blowup, exp_008). Priority 1 from STRATEGY.md is now closed off.

idea: Grid-harmonic features v2: widen ACF/cepstrum lag lookup from exact-rounded single sample to a +/-2 sample window max, fixing potential lag-rounding brittleness especially at high candidate f0 where 1 sample = large relative freq error
notes: targeted robustness fix to exp_005's grid feature family, per STRATEGY.md priority 2 (feature engineering on grid family itself) | Probe (same 300/100 subsample as exp_005, N_SPLITS=2, 65 classes, identical LGBM config): grid v1 (exact-rounded lag) OOF=0.5876 vs grid v2 (+/-2 sample windowed max lag) OOF=0.5670, delta -0.0206. Widening the ACF/cepstrum lag window HURTS -- likely trades precision for recall in a regime where the exact lag was already discriminative; a wider window picks up neighboring-candidate peaks too, blurring the per-candidate signal that separates adjacent pitch classes. Falsifies the lag-rounding-brittleness hypothesis; not promoting to full. Closes off this specific robustness-fix angle for the grid feature family.

idea: Temporal-windowed harmonic-evidence features: split each clip into 3 equal time windows, score candidate-f0 harmonic energy per window, add max/std-across-windows as new columns concatenated with exp_005's grid features -- tests whether pitch evidence varies meaningfully across a 6.6s clip (onset vs sustain) beyond what full-clip-only features capture
notes: priority 2 STRATEGY.md direction, additive features not a replacement | Probe (same 300/100 subsample, N_SPLITS=2, 65 classes, identical LGBM config): grid-only OOF=0.5876 vs grid+temporal-windowed (405+162=567 feats) OOF=0.5704, delta -0.0172. Adding max/std-across-3-windows harmonic scores HURTS, same pattern as exp_007 (grid+generic) and exp_014 (widened lag) -- LGBM's split budget gets diluted by additional correlated/noisier features rather than gaining new signal, at this ~300-row probe scale. Not promoting to full. This is the 3rd consecutive negative feature-engineering/ensembling attempt on the grid family this block (exp_013 LGBM+MLP, exp_014 widened lag, exp_015 windowed) -- per C9, pivot away from 'add more features/models on top of grid+LGBM' entirely for this block; STRATEGY.md priority 3 (run /research) is next.

idea: MLP-alone on FULL 2330-row/82-class grid features, single stratified 80/20 holdout, vs LGBM on identical split -- resolves whether the existing 'MLP is far worse' verdict (probe_mlp_grid.py, tiny 300-row subsample) is a probe-fidelity artifact like exp_010's LGBM hparam reversal, or a real full-scale fact
notes: research pass rejected SHS-decay-weighting as subsumed by existing harm_low/harm_high features (C3 explicit rejection, see RESEARCH.md); redirected to this instead since the only MLP evidence on record used the same misleading tiny-subsample fidelity that burned exp_010 | MLP-alone (StandardScaler+MLPClassifier 128x64) full-scale single 80/20 holdout on grid features: acc 0.9227 vs LGBM 0.9013 on identical split, delta +0.0215. REVERSES tiny-probe verdict (probe_mlp_grid.py: MLP OOF 0.2096 vs LGBM 0.5876 on 300-row subsample) -- same probe-fidelity-artifact pattern as exp_010/exp_012. MLP is also ~80x faster to fit (7s vs 564s) at this feature count. Model-family diversification on grid features is viable after all; next: proper 3-fold CV to confirm vs single-holdout noise, then LGBM+MLP blend at full scale.

idea: Full 3-fold StratifiedKFold CV (not single holdout) comparing LGBM-alone, MLP-alone, and LGBM+MLP soft-vote blend on grid features -- confirms exp_016's single-holdout MLP>LGBM result (+0.0215) isn't holdout noise, and gives real full-scale evidence for the LGBM+MLP blend that exp_008/exp_013 couldn't properly test (SVM blew up cost, and the ensemble-vs-LGBM-only probe was on the same misleading tiny subsample)
notes: direct follow-up to exp_016 per RESEARCH.md recommended priority 2 | Full 3-fold CV: LGBM-only OOF=0.9090, MLP-only OOF=0.9288 (confirms exp_016 reversal at proper CV, not holdout noise), best blend w_lgb=0.4 OOF=0.9476 -- delta +0.0386 vs current best (sub_020 LGBM-only 0.9086/0.9090). Big new best; building full-train-fit blend submission next.

idea: exp_018: augment rare-tail classes (<10 samples/class) with fold-safe additive-noise + time-stretch copies (no pitch-shift, since pitch is the label) before grid-feature extraction; compare full CV accuracy and balanced_accuracy vs exp_005 baseline
notes: research pass block 3; augmentation must happen strictly inside each CV fold's training split to avoid leaking augmented copies of a rare clip into its own validation fold | Fold-safe rare-class (<10/class, 14/82) augmentation (additive-noise+time-stretch, no pitch-shift), single 80/20 holdout: overall acc +0.0065, balanced_acc +0.0189, rare-class-subset acc +0.0625 (0.75->0.8125). Below predicted_delta 0.02 on overall acc but clearly positive esp. on the metric it targets (balanced_acc, rare-class subset); promoting to full 3-fold CV. | Full 3-fold CV: baseline OOF acc=0.9090 balanced_acc=0.8413 -> augmented OOF acc=0.9185 balanced_acc=0.8896 (delta acc +0.0094, delta balanced_acc +0.0483). Rare-class(<10/class) subset acc 0.5342->0.8219 (+0.2877). Matches probe direction (probe was +0.0065/+0.0189/+0.0625), promoted. | full 3-fold CV: baseline OOF acc=0.9090 balanced_acc=0.8413, augmented OOF acc=0.9185 balanced_acc=0.8896, delta acc +0.0094 balanced_acc +0.0483, rare-class-subset acc +0.2877 (0.5342->0.8219). Fold-safe (augmentation inside each fold's train split only). Confirms probe direction at full fidelity.

idea: Multi-seed MLP averaging (5 seeds) blended with LGBM at w_lgb=0.4 (exp_017's best blend weight) on grid features -- MLP fit is ~5s/fold so 5-seed averaging is nearly free; tests whether seed-variance reduction on the MLP component adds further signal on top of the LGBM+MLP blend, per gemini's independently-validated multi-seed-MLP pattern (their sub_009+ series) applied to our shift-robust grid feature family instead of their shift-prone generic features.
notes: Follow-up to exp_017 (LGBM+MLP blend, OOF 0.9476, submitted as sub_022); probe via full-data single 80/20 holdout per exp_012's row-subsampling-probe-fidelity lesson (not a small subsample). | Multi-seed(5) MLP averaging blended w_lgb=0.4 vs single-seed blend, full-data single 80/20 holdout: single-seed blend acc=0.9525, 5-seed-avg blend acc=0.9546, delta +0.0022 (below predicted +0.01 but positive, nearly free at ~25s extra). Promoting to full 3-fold CV. | Full 3-fold CV (reused exp_017 saved oof_lgb; refit only MLP x5 seeds per fold): MLP 5-seed-avg alone jumps 0.9288->0.9545 (seed-variance reduction works great for MLP-alone), but blended with LGBM at w_lgb=0.4 the result is flat-to-negative: single-seed blend 0.9476 vs 5-seed-avg blend 0.9468, delta -0.0009. Reverses the tiny positive probe delta (+0.0022, single 80/20 holdout). Not promoting -- current best blend (sub_022 config) stands unchanged.

idea: Shift-aware check on MLP component specifically: exp_006 found LGBM-alone grid features have almost no accuracy gap between test-like and non-test-like train quartiles (gap -0.0095), but sub_022 (LGBM+MLP blend, CV 0.9476) scored WORSE on public LB (0.87931) than sub_020 (LGBM-only, CV 0.9086, LB 0.91954) -- a CV-up/LB-down sign disagreement. Hypothesis: MLP is more shift-fragile than LGBM on the same grid features (different inductive bias / extrapolation behavior under distribution shift), even though both share the same underlying features that exp_006 checked only for LGBM.
notes: Reuses exp_006's adversarial P(is_test) classifier methodology; apply it separately to oof_lgb_exp017.npy and oof_mlp_exp017.npy (already saved) to see if the accuracy gap (plain vs most-test-like quartile) differs between the two model families. This is a methodology/diagnosis experiment, not an accuracy-improvement one -- success is explaining the sub_022 CV-LB divergence, C10 write-back required either way. | Reused exp_017's saved OOF arrays + exp_006-style adversarial P(is_test) quartile diagnostic, applied separately to LGBM-only, MLP-only, and the blend (w_lgb=0.4). Result: gap_lgb=-0.0087, gap_mlp=-0.0129, gap_blend=-0.0078 (blend has the SMALLEST gap of the three, all close to exp_006's LGBM reference -0.0095). MLP-more-shift-fragile hypothesis NOT confirmed (diff is within noise, and doesn't even point the right direction to explain the blend). This is the key finding: the adversarial-quartile shift diagnostic (exp_006/exp_020 methodology) predicts NO meaningful shift-accuracy interaction for any of these three, yet sub_022 (the actual blend submission) measured a real -0.04 CV-to-LB drop vs sub_020. The diagnostic fails to explain the observed CV-LB divergence -- treat this as a methodology limitation, not a cleared bill of health. Flagging for coordinator/other agents: a clean adversarial-quartile gap on grid features is NOT sufficient evidence that a submission's CV will transfer to LB; the mechanism behind sub_022's drop remains unexplained. Recommend treating any further grid-feature blend submissions with a wider CV-LB safety margin until this is understood, and continuing to prefer sub_020 (LGBM-only, LB-confirmed 0.91954) as the safer pick over sub_022 (CV-best but LB-unconfirmed-negative) for any 'pick a final' decision.

idea: exp_021: logistic-regression stacking meta-learner on [oof_lgb_exp017, oof_mlp_exp017] concatenated proba (164-dim), 3-fold CV on the meta-learner itself (nested, using the same base-model OOF splits) -- replaces exp_017's fixed scalar blend weight (w_lgb=0.4) with a learned per-class combination, testing whether a richer combiner captures class-specific LGBM-vs-MLP reliability differences that a single global weight can't
notes: Reuses exp_017's saved OOF arrays directly, no refitting of base models needed -- meta-learner fit is near-free (2330x164 logistic regression). Given exp_019's finding that even a fully-orthogonal signal-boost (5-seed MLP averaging) didn't move the blend, this tests a different lever (combiner richness, not base-model quality). | Logreg stacking meta-learner (164-dim: oof_lgb+oof_mlp concat) on exp_017's saved OOF arrays, nested 3-fold CV (same StratifiedKFold(3,42) row groups): meta acc=0.9348 vs fixed-weight blend (w_lgb=0.4) acc=0.9476, delta -0.0129. This already used full 2330-row data with proper CV (no subsample step applicable for this idea), so treating as final result. A learned combiner overfits relative to the 1-parameter scalar blend at this row count (~1550 train rows/fold for an 82-class multinomial over 164 features). Not promoting; simple scalar blend weight remains best.

idea: exp_022: descriptive analysis (no retraining) of sub_020 vs sub_022 test-set prediction disagreements -- checks whether the LGBM+MLP blend systematically shifts predictions away from rare classes vs LGBM-only, as a candidate mechanism for the measured CV-up/LB-down divergence that exp_020's adversarial-quartile diagnostic could not explain
notes: Purely descriptive, no true test labels available so this is a hypothesis-generating check, not a confirmatory one -- follow-up to exp_020 | Descriptive test-set comparison, sub_020 (LGBM-only) vs sub_022 (LGBM+MLP blend): 80/583 (13.7%) test predictions disagree. Of disagreements, sub_020 picks a rare(<10-train-sample) class 21.25% of the time vs sub_022 only 8.75% -- the blend systematically shifts disagreement-point predictions AWAY from rare classes and toward mid-frequency classes (per-class swing table: classes 3/5/8/58/47/33/28/40 lose blend-share, classes 69/38/11/14/55/30/31/12 gain it). Both submissions under-predict rare classes relative to train's already-low 3.13% rare rate (020=5.15%, 022=3.43% pred rate) and over-predict overall relative to train's class balance (L1 dist from train prior: 020=0.410, 022=0.365 -- blend is closer to train prior, not further). This is a plausible mechanism for the CV-up/LB-down divergence: MLP's smoother decision boundary corrects LGBM's rare-class overcalls in a way that helps on CV (which shares train's class balance) but may hurt on the actual test set if real test rare-class rate differs from train's, or if LGBM's rare-class picks were disproportionately correct despite being rare. Cannot confirm causally without true test labels -- this is a hypothesis, not proof, but is the first concrete lead beyond exp_020's ruled-out shift-fragility explanation. Actionable: treat any future blend that further smooths class-balance toward the train prior with extra suspicion on this dataset; LGBM-only's willingness to call rare classes may be a feature, not a bug, on the real test distribution.

idea: exp_023: shift-aware blend-weight search between exp_017's oof_lgb and oof_mlp (grid features), scored by exp_006/exp_020's adversarial-quartile-gap method (not plain OOF). Test whether shift-aware scoring would have picked a weight closer to sub_020's w_lgb=1.0 instead of plain-OOF's w_lgb=0.4 that was submitted as sub_022 and lost -0.04 on LB. Reuses saved OOF arrays, no retraining -- cheap.
notes: Direct follow-up to exp_020 (falsified MLP-more-fragile) and exp_011 (falsified shift-aware-picks-different-weight, but for a DIFFERENT blend pair: grid-vs-generic, not LGBM-vs-MLP within grid). If shift-aware topq_acc peaks near w=1.0 while plain OOF peaks near w=0.4, that's the first evidence shift-aware scoring would have avoided the sub_022 LB drop before submitting. | Shift-aware weight search (topq_acc, exp_006/exp_020 method) between exp_017's oof_lgb/oof_mlp: topq_acc PEAKS at w=0.4 (0.9554), same as plain OOF -- NOT at w=1.0 (topq=0.9177, worse). This falsifies the hypothesis that shift-aware/most-test-like-quartile scoring would have flagged sub_022's blend as risky before submitting; it agrees with plain OOF and would have picked the SAME weight that lost -0.04 on LB. Strengthens MEMORY_CANDIDATES row 11 (adversarial-quartile-gap diagnostic gives a clean bill of health it shouldn't) with a second, harder test: this time on a direct weight-search decision, not just a per-model gap check. The real mechanism behind sub_022's LB drop remains unexplained by any in-train diagnostic tried so far (exp_006, exp_020, exp_023 all clean/agreeing).

idea: exp_024: per-feature train/test shift diagnostic on grid features -- compute univariate adversarial AUC per feature column (410 cols), cross-reference against LGBM feature_importances_ from a single full-train fit; identify features that are BOTH high-shift AND high-importance as candidates to drop, to find a concrete mechanism/fix for the still-unexplained sub_022 CV-up/LB-down drop (exp_006/020/023 all found no explanation via model/blend-level diagnostics)

Correlation(shift_auc, lgbm_importance) across all 405 features is -0.28 (weakly NEGATIVE, not positive) -- the model is not disproportionately relying on the most-shifted features. Falsifies the 'drop high-shift+high-importance features' mechanism as an explanation/fix for sub_022's CV-up/LB-down drop. exp_006/020/022/023/024 have now all failed to find the mechanism via model/blend/feature-level diagnostics.

idea: exp_027: prior-correct exp_025's augmented LGBM+MLP blend OOF probabilities (divide by train class prior^alpha) to directly test exp_022's hypothesis that the blend over-smooths toward train class balance at the expense of rare classes -- reuses exp_025's saved OOF arrays, no retraining

KILLED: prior-correction was the right idea for the pre-augmentation blend (sub_022) but augmentation already substantially addressed the same failure mode; stacking both is redundant-to-harmful. idea: Frozen pretrained CREPE (or similar pitch-tracking CNN) checkpoint used as a FEATURE EXTRACTOR (not trained from scratch) on top of/alongside the grid-harmonic features -- bigger lift than any remaining axis (LGBM capacity, blend-weight, meta-learner stacking, feature widening, temporal windowing, prior-correction are all closed off per C9). Requires network access to fetch a checkpoint and COMPETITION.md/RULES.md do not explicitly address external-pretrained-model use -- confirm rules allow it before running.
notes: STRATEGY.md fresh-angle option (a); check external-pretrained-model rule ambiguity first, this is a bigger lift than anything tried so far this competition | torchcrepe (model='tiny', frozen pretrained, no fine-tuning) 5th-maxpool embedding, mean+std pooled over time to a 512-dim vector per clip, concatenated onto exp_005's grid-harmonic features (917 total feats). CREPE-embedding-alone OOF=0.1649, i.e. -0.4227 below grid-only -- the pretrained pitch-CNN embedding is far weaker than the domain-specific harmonic-evidence-grid features for this task's specific Pitch_ID taxonomy, and adding it as extra columns just adds noise dimensions for LGBM at this row count (405->917 feats on 291 rows). Consistent with the pre-registered risk in PREDICTION.md and the /research pass's prior rejection of raw-waveform CNN pitch estimators (need far more per-class labeled data than 82 classes/~28 samples/class average provides) -- CREPE is trained for monophonic single-f0 estimation, not this competition's missing-fundamental Pitch_ID classification, and transfers poorly even as a frozen feature extractor. Killed at probe (C9); closes off the pretrained-CNN axis -- this was the last item in the queue with a nonzero remaining lever per prior blocks' framing.

idea: exp_029: frozen pretrained PANNs (Cnn14, AudioSet-trained general audio-event CNN, NOT pitch-specific like exp_028's CREPE) used as a feature extractor -- 2048-dim global-pooled embedding concatenated onto exp_005 grid-harmonic features. Different mechanism from exp_028 (timbral/spectral general audio tagging vs single-f0 pitch tracking); research pass found general-purpose AudioSet embeddings often transfer better than task-specific ones on small datasets.
notes: extraction running in background (extract_panns_embed.py), probe script probe_exp029_panns.py to be written once features land | torchcrepe's negative result generalizes: frozen PANNs (Cnn14, AudioSet 527-class general audio-event tagger, NOT pitch-specific) 2048-dim global-pooled embedding, concatenated onto exp_005 grid features (2453 total), same 291-row/65-class probe subsample+LGBM config. grid-only OOF=0.5876 vs grid+PANNs OOF=0.5739, delta -0.0137. Two architecturally distinct pretrained audio embeddings (CREPE: pitch-specific single-f0 tracking; PANNs: general-purpose AudioSet event tagging) BOTH fail on this task via different mechanisms -- neither narrow pitch-tracking nor broad timbral/event embeddings transfer to this competition's designed 82-class Pitch_ID taxonomy on missing-fundamental harmonic-complex tones. Likely because this task needs fine pitch-height precision (semitone-level, 82 classes) that neither pretrained model's training objective preserves (CREPE collapses to a single ambiguous f0 estimate on these deliberately-fundamental-absent clips; PANNs pools for coarse sound-event identity, not pitch height). Recorded via scheduler.py, KILLED at probe (C9). Closes the external-pretrained-audio-embedding family as a lever on this dataset (2/2 failed, different mechanisms, no reason to expect a 3rd architecture would differ).

idea: exp_030: fit MLP-alone (no LGBM blend) full-train on grid features, predict test, save submission CSV. notes: prep for tomorrow: claude daily submission cap (2/2) already hit today per registry, so this is prep only, not a submission this block | MLP-alone full-train fit + test predict done (grid features, exp_005 family). If MLP-alone beats sub_020 on LB despite lower CV than the blends, that points to blend-architecture-specific fragility (e.g. compounding two models' shift errors); if it does worse than sub_020, that points to MLP-family fragility specifically, consistent with (but not proven by) exp_020's slightly-larger-but-inconclusive MLP shift gap (-0.0129 vs LGBM's -0.0087).

idea: exp_031: descriptive (no retraining, no true labels) three-way test-prediction comparison -- sub_020 (LGBM-alone, LB-best 0.91954) vs sub_024 (LGBM+MLP blend+aug, LB 0.89655) vs exp_030 (MLP-alone, not yet LB-tested). Checks whether MLP-alone's disagreement pattern vs LGBM-alone looks more like the blend's pattern (shared MLP-driven bias) or is a distinct third pattern, and repeats exp_022's rare-class/class-prior-distance diagnostic for all three to see where MLP-alone falls on that axis before deciding whether it's worth spending a submission slot on tomorrow.
notes: cheap, reuses existing submission CSVs, follow-up to exp_022/exp_030 | Descriptive 3-way test-prediction comparison (no true labels, no retraining) among sub_020 (LGBM-alone, LB-best 0.91954), sub_024 (LGBM+MLP-aug blend w_lgb=0.4, LB 0.89655), exp_030 (MLP-alone, not yet LB-tested). KEY FINDING: MLP-alone's predictions are far closer to the blend than to LGBM-alone -- disagreement rates: lgbm-vs-blend 14.58%, lgbm-vs-mlp 21.78%, blend-vs-mlp only 9.43%. On the 85 rows where LGBM-alone disagrees with the blend, MLP-alone sides with the blend 87.06% of the time and with LGBM-alone only 2.35% of the time. Mechanistic explanation: w_lgb=0.4 means the blend is 60% MLP-weighted, so it is MLP-dominated, not a balanced 50/50 mix -- the blend's predictions are structurally close to a pure MLP model. rare_rate/prior_l1: lgbm_only=0.0515/0.410, blend=0.0326/0.359, mlp_only=0.0326/0.378 -- MLP-alone matches the blend's rare-class/prior-distance profile almost exactly, both far from LGBM-alone's. This makes a concrete, falsifiable prediction for exp_030's still-untested LB score: since blend (MLP-dominated) underperforms LGBM-alone on LB (0.877-0.897 vs 0.920), and MLP-alone's predictions are 90%+ similar to the blend's, MLP-alone should ALSO underperform LGBM-alone on LB by a similar margin -- if true, this finally identifies MODEL FAMILY (MLP's inductive bias under this dataset's shift) as the mechanism, not blend-interaction, resolving what exp_006/020/022/023/024/026/027 (6 prior diagnostics) could not explain. Recorded as a full/no-retrain descriptive result (delta 0.0, methodology/diagnostic, not an accuracy change).

idea: exp_032: full-train LGBM(aug)+MLP(aug) blend at w_lgb=0.8 (LGBM-dominated, vs sub_022/024's MLP-dominated w_lgb=0.4) -- direct hedge candidate motivated by exp_031's finding that blend predictions track weight share almost 1:1 (at w_lgb=0.4 the blend agrees with MLP-alone 90.6% of the time and LGBM-alone only 85.4%), and LGBM-alone is the only LB-confirmed-good submission.
